trigger_keywords: true or false in version comparison line
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The exact value is the result of the comparison logic (either 'true' or 'false') based on input tags.
Number runs are converted to integers for numeric comparison, while non-number runs are compared lexicographically. Comparison proceeds left-to-right across all runs.

trigger_keywords: equal comparison result for malformed tags
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: In this case, the output indicates the tags cannot be ordered; the comparison result is effectively equal or no-order, preventing any strict less-than or greater-than classification.

context_pointers.vague_reference: align with the standard Docker suffix patterns defined in C014
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Host comparison must match the exact 'docker.io' or 'docker.com' directly or appear as a suffix to the hostname, failing otherwise.

context_pointers.vague_reference: utilize the splitting logic for multi-segment paths as per C020
context_pointers.actual_meaning: All but the last segment constitute the repository name, accessed by slicing the path string excluding the final slash and its contents.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_version_parsing.json.description: Parse a container image tag into a three-part numeric version (major, minor, patch) plus a free-form metadata suffix. A leading 'v' is optional. Missing numeric components default to 0. Any text that follows the patch number (or any text that prevents a leading numeric match) is preserved verbatim as metadata. Tags that do not start with a number yield a zero version and carry the whole string as metadata.

feature2_version_comparison.json.description: Determine whether one image tag represents an older version than another, returning a boolean ordering result. Comparison first weighs the numeric major/minor/patch triple; ties are broken by tokenising the metadata suffix into alternating number/word segments and comparing them left-to-right (numbers compared numerically, words lexicographically). A tag with no metadata is considered newer than the same numeric version with a pre-release metadata suffix. Empty tags order purely by length. Two arbitrary non-numeric strings never order as less-than.

feature3_1_host_match_docker.json.description: Decide whether a registry host string belongs to the Docker Hub registry. A host matches only when it is exactly docker.io or docker.com, or any subdomain ending in .docker.io / .docker.com. Strings that merely contain the substring (prefix or suffix glued on) do not match, and an empty host never matches.

feature3_2_host_match_gcr.json.description: Decide whether a registry host string belongs to the GCR registry. A host matches only when it is exactly gcr.io or any subdomain ending in .gcr.io. Strings that merely contain the substring (prefix or suffix glued on) do not match, and an empty host never matches.

feature3_3_host_match_quay.json.description: Decide whether a registry host string belongs to the Quay registry. A host matches only when it is exactly quay.io or any subdomain ending in .quay.io. Strings that merely contain the substring (prefix or suffix glued on) do not match, and an empty host never matches.

feature4_1_path_split_docker.json.description: Split a Docker Hub image path into a repository and image name. A bare single segment is treated as an official image under the implicit 'library' repository. When the path has two or more segments, only the last two are used: the second-to-last is the repository and the last is the image (any leading registry segments are discarded).

feature4_2_path_split_gcr.json.description: Split a GCR image path into a repository and image name. A bare single segment is treated as an image under the implicit 'google-containers' repository. When the path contains slashes, everything before the last slash becomes the repository (preserving intermediate segments) and the final segment is the image.

feature4_3_path_split_quay.json.description: Split a Quay image path into a repository and image name. A bare single segment is treated as the repository with an empty image. When the path contains slashes, everything before the last slash becomes the repository (preserving intermediate segments) and the final segment is the image.

feature5_url_routing.json.description: Route a full image URL to the registry that should handle it, also reporting the host and the remaining path. The first slash-delimited segment is treated as a candidate host: if it matches the Docker, GCR, or Quay host rules the URL is routed there with that host and the remainder as path. A URL with no slash, or whose leading segment matches no known registry host, falls back to the Docker registry with an empty host and the entire original string as the path.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'parse_version' and input contains 'tag' string
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain exactly 5 lines with specific key=value format

_constraints.input_condition: Tag string starts with optional 'v' followed by digits or dot
_constraints.output_rule: Leading 'v' is stripped from the version components but preserved in the 'tag' output line

_constraints.input_condition: Tag string contains dot-separated numbers
_constraints.output_rule: Extract up to 3 leading dot-separated integers as major/minor/patch. Missing components default to 0.

_constraints.input_condition: Tag string portion immediately following the matched third numeric patch group
_constraints.output_rule: This remaining text becomes the 'metadata' value. If no non-version text follows, metadata is empty.

_constraints.input_condition: Tag string does not begin with a number (e.g., starts with 'v' alone or text like 'hello-')
_constraints.output_rule: Major/Minor/Patch must be 0/0/0. The entire original tag string goes to metadata.

_constraints.input_condition: Patch number ends with a dot (e.g., '1.3.' implied by context of mixed strings)
_constraints.output_rule: The metadata capture starts immediately after the patch integer. Dots are preserved.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'compare_versions' and input contains 'first' and 'second' tag strings
_constraints.output_rule: Output must have 3 lines: echo original tags, then 'less_than=<bool>'

_constraints.input_condition: Either 'first' or 'second' tag is an empty string
_constraints.output_rule: Compare strictly by string length. If lengths differ, shorter is 'less_than=true'. If lengths identical, 'less_than=false'.

_constraints.input_condition: Numeric major/minor/patch triple is identical between first and second
_constraints.output_rule: If 'first' has no metadata suffix and 'second' has a non-empty metadata suffix, 'less_than=false' (release is newer).

_constraints.input_condition: Primary version triple is identical; compare metadata suffixes lexically using alternates (number/run vs non-num/run)
_constraints.output_rule: Number runs compared numerically. Non-number runs compared lexicographically. Runs align left-to-right.

_constraints.input_condition: Metadata run contains multiple digits (e.g., 'r39' vs 'r9')
_constraints.output_rule: Compare the numeric value of the run. 'r9' < 'r39' because 9 < 39.

_constraints.input_condition: Neither 'first' nor 'second' resolve to a valid version triple (no starting numbers, no metadata separators)
_constraints.output_rule: The two tags must NEVER be strictly less/greater than each other.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'match_host', registry is 'docker', and host is not empty
_constraints.output_rule: Returns 3 lines. 'match=true' if host ends with '.docker.io' or '.docker.com'. 'match=false' otherwise.

_constraints.input_condition: Host string is exactly 'docker.io' or 'docker.com'
_constraints.output_rule: Treats substring matches like 'foodocker.io' as false. Only exact match or suffix match returns true.

_constraints.input_condition: Host matches gcr suffix but includes version/channel (e.g. 'us.gcr.io', 'k8s.gcr.io')
_constraints.output_rule: Matches the canonical domain pattern (.gcr.io), allowing subdomain prefixes.

_constraints.input_condition: Host matches quay.io canonical domain
_constraints.output_rule: Returns true if structure ends in '.quay.io'. Returns false if glued to other text.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'split_path', registry 'docker', and path has exactly 1 segment
_constraints.output_rule: Output: repo='library', image=<path>

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'split_path', registry 'docker', and path has 2+ segments
_constraints.output_rule: Output: repo=second to last segment, image=last segment. Discard all preceding segments.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'split_path', registry 'gcr', and path has exactly 1 segment
_constraints.output_rule: Output: repo='google-containers', image=<path>

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'split_path', registry 'gcr', and path has 2+ segments
_constraints.output_rule: Output: repo=everything before last slash, image=last segment.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'split_path', registry 'quay', and path has exactly 1 segment
_constraints.output_rule: Output: repo=<path>, image='' (empty string)

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'split_path', registry 'quay', and path has 2+ segments
_constraints.output_rule: Output: repo=everything before last slash, image=last segment.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'route_url' and input contains 'url' string
_constraints.output_rule: Output must expose href='url', path='<registry_host>', where registry is inferred from host

_constraints.input_condition: URL start segment matches 'docker.io', 'docker.com', 'gcr.io', 'us.gcr.io', or 'quay.io'
_constraints.output_rule: Route to inferred registry ('docker', 'gcr', or 'quay') and host. Path is remainder after host.

_constraints.input_condition: URL start segment does not match any known registry host (e.g. 'nginx', 'random.io')
_constraints.output_rule: Route to 'docker' registry. If host hostname itself is not docker.com or docker.io, the initial host string in path is set to empty.

_constraints.input_condition: URL has no slashes or is a single identifier (no match above C025)
_constraints.output_rule: Treat the whole URL as the path. Default to 'docker' registry. Host is empty string.

_constraints.input_condition: ALL actions
_constraints.output_rule: Output lines must be printed in the exact sequence defined by each JSON test case's expected_output block. Deviation implies failure.
